description:
Configure Amazon Cognito as Apoth's patient identity provider.

**Scope**
- Define user pool, app client, domains/callbacks, password policy, account recovery, email verification, and MFA posture for launch.
- Add a thin auth facade at `src/lib/auth.ts` for session lookup, current-user access, and protected-route helpers so product code does not depend directly on Cognito SDK details.
- Document local/staging/prod Cognito configuration and environment variables.
- Keep auth data in Cognito. Do not introduce better-auth, Clerk, Supabase Auth, Postgres auth tables, or Redis session caches without a new architecture decision.

**Acceptance**
- A test user can sign up, verify email, sign in, sign out, and retrieve a server-side session through the facade.
- All app auth call sites go through `src/lib/auth.ts` or a local wrapper built on it.
- Setup docs make clear which identifiers are safe to store in DynamoDB (`cognito_sub`) and which data remains in Cognito.
title: Cognito auth provider setup
